Above the doorway, a carved wooden sign displays a lantern descending into darkness-the Guild's crest-positioned over door frames worn smooth from years of use by armored hands.

Guild guards in weathered leather armor stand at attention near the entrance, their watchful eyes scanning the crowd for troublemakers and those seeking to bypass the sanctioned quest boards within. The weak winter light casts long shadows across the scene, and the surrounding buildings appear humble in comparison to the guildhall's sturdy, imposing presence.

[Title: The Sunken Labyrinth of Valdros; Tags: dungeon crawler, dark fantasy, exploration, treasure hunting; Genre: fantasy, adventure, horror-adjacent]

A rain-warped wooden notice board stands mounted on corroded iron brackets beside the heavy oak entrance of the Guildhall, the board itself gray and swollen from countless winters of exposure. Layers of curling parchment sheets overlap one another in thick, disorganized sheaves, their edges browned and brittle from moisture. Some notices have torn free at the corners, fluttering weakly in the cold draft, while others have been pinned directly over older announcements, creating a stratified record of expeditions past. Faded ink describes missing companions with crude charcoal sketches, hastily scrawled warnings of newly discovered hazards in the Sunken Labyrinth, and advertisements seeking able-bodied adventurers for upcoming descents into the depths.

The Guildhall's weathered stone wall rises behind the board, its surface stained with centuries of grime and lichen growth. Iron lantern brackets flank the entrance, their flames guttering behind smoky glass panels. Carved into the lintel above the doorway, the Guild's crest-a lantern descending into darkness-has been worn smooth by generations of passing adventurers. A few discarded parchment scraps and broken sealing wax litter the flagstones beneath the board, pressed into crevices by boot traffic and winter weather. The simple grey background eliminates all distraction, presenting this humble artifact of dungeon-crawling commerce as a monument to hope, desperation, and the endless hunger for treasure that draws souls down into Valdros's eternal night.

The post's weathered construction speaks to countless adventurers who have passed beneath its meager illumination, returning from the depths in the bitter hours before dawn when the air hangs cold and heavy over Dusthollow's streets.

A weathered stone bench sits centered against a neutral grey background, its surface worn smooth by decades of touch from grieving hands. The dark granite is densely carved with dozens of names etched in varying scripts and sizes-some freshly chiseled, others faded nearly to oblivion-creating a tapestry of remembrance across every available surface. Fine cracks web through the stone where time and moisture have worked their damage, and a subtle layer of dust settles into the deeper grooves of the carved letters.

Small offerings cluster in the natural crevices and corners of the bench: tarnished copper coins, brittle dried flowers with curled brown petals, a tarnished silver ring, fragments of faded ribbon, and tiny carved wooden figures. A few candle stubs sit in puddles of hardened wax at the bench's base, and someone has left a small leather pouch containing what might be herbs or ashes. The accumulated mementos create small mounds of devotion, their muted colors blending with the somber grey stone.

The bench captures the quiet weight of accumulated grief, standing alone as a silent monument to those who descended into the Sunken Labyrinth and never emerged. Wear patterns on the armrests speak to the countless hours survivors have spent seated here, while the offerings-some ancient and crumbling, others recent enough to still hold color-testify to a tradition of remembrance that spans generations of Dusthollow's adventuring community.
